description: This work features an unusually muted palette for O’Keeffe’s Southwestern paintings. While she painted numerous images of the region’s rounded gray hills, here she depicts a low, sandy crest that reminded her of her husband, Alfred Stieglitz. She wrote to him: “When I see the country in its silvery beauty and forbidding blackness in my memory—it is so often almost as if I see you too—your silvery hair and grey clothes and black cape."
title: The Black Place
